_readme: Copy this entire folder, rename it (e.g. ch02), then edit this file. Delete any _readme fields before building.
title: Running on Empty: Chronic Sleep Debt
series_subtitle: A Field Guide for Frontline Folks and Their Families
cover_tagline: Chronic sleep debt erodes judgment long before you notice it.

sections.heading: Sleep Debt Is a Performance Injury

content.text: Chronic sleep debt is not just feeling tired. It is cumulative neurological load that reduces reaction time, emotional control, and decision quality.

items: Lower pain tolerance and higher inflammation
Stronger cravings for sugar, caffeine, and alcohol

content.text: The issue is not only hours slept. It is rhythm disruption. Rotating shifts, adrenaline carryover, and inconsistent wake times fragment the recovery cycle.

items: Alcohol helps sleep onset but worsens sleep quality
Phone and TV light suppresses melatonin

lines: Why this works: fixed wake anchors and evening light control stabilize circadian signals and improve sleep efficiency over 1-3 weeks.

items.text: keep it consistent within a practical range, even after rough nights.
20 minutes of low light, low input, and no work decisions.
cut intake at least 8 hours before intended sleep.
10-30 minutes when needed, but avoid late naps that steal nighttime sleep.

items: Define one protected sleep block as non-negotiable.
Set household noise and interruption rules during recovery windows.
Use a simple sign or message for do-not-disturb periods.
Schedule connection time after recovery to prevent resentment.

sections.heading: When to Evaluate Apnea, Insomnia, or Burnout

items: Loud snoring, witnessed pauses, or morning headaches
Regular 3 a.m. wakeups with inability to return to sleep
Dependence on alcohol or sedatives to initiate sleep
Persistent daytime impairment despite sleep effort

content.text: Track wake time, caffeine cutoff, pre-sleep routine, and next-day function for one week. Focus on consistency, not perfection.

lines: One behavior that protects tomorrow's energy.

worksheets.title: Worksheet: Running on Empty - Sleep Debt Recovery Plan
worksheets.intro: Use this to connect sleep behaviors with next-day performance and mood.

question_groups.label: What sleep disruptors showed up this week?
question_groups.hint: Late caffeine, shift changes, alcohol, rumination, screens, pain, nightmares.

question_groups.label: What did poor sleep cost you the next day?
question_groups.hint: Reaction time, patience, pain, attention, errors, conflict.

question_groups.label: What two sleep-protection actions are non-negotiable this week?
question_groups.hint: Pick realistic actions you can repeat even on hard shifts.

numbered_prompts.hint: What time did you wake and how consistent was it?
What time was your last caffeine intake?
What did you do in the last 20-30 minutes before bed?
Rate energy, focus, and emotional control.

worksheets.closing_note: Treat sleep as mission-critical maintenance, not optional recovery.

worksheets.title: Worksheet: Running on Empty - Household Sleep Agreement
worksheets.intro: Use this with your household to protect one daily recovery block without creating resentment.

question_groups.label: Protected sleep block
question_groups.hint: What specific time window is protected this week?

question_groups.label: Do-not-disturb rules
question_groups.hint: List noise, phone, and interruption boundaries during the block.

question_groups.label: Post-recovery reconnection plan
question_groups.hint: How and when will you reconnect so family does not feel shut out?

numbered_prompts.hint: What phrase or sign means recovery block is active?
What alternate recovery window will you use?
Did this plan improve mood, patience, and function?

worksheets.closing_note: Sleep plans work when they are visible, specific, and shared.

paragraphs: You cannot out-tough chronic sleep debt. It will eventually tax your health, your patience, and your judgment.
Protecting sleep is not selfish. It is how you stay effective for the people who depend on you.
closing.highlight: Consistency beats heroics. One protected sleep habit repeated daily changes everything.
